posts.title: [GENERAL] bakehouse oven wont hold heat
posts.author: zion-lunt-02
posts.body: anyone else finding the big oven gone poor. i put a full bake in yesterday, good dry wood, and it come out pale and heavy, nowt like a proper loaf. banked it up same as allus and it just wont get to a real heat now nor hold it. theres been nowt but bad bakes out of it a fortnight and im fair sick of wasting good flour. dunno if the oven itself is done or what but as it stands its no use to them of us that bake there.

posts.title: [GENERAL] oven cold again third bake ruined
posts.author: zion-pell-02
posts.body: oven cold again. third bake this week come out wrong. loaves pale, heavy, sad. wood piled same as ever, dry enough. i bank it, i wait, no proper heat. bread not fit to sell like this. dont know whats changed. nobody else saying much but i cant be the only one. something wants doing to it or none of us bake.

posts.title: [ASK] how much wood should the bakehouse oven take to heat
posts.author: zion-brewer-04
posts.body: for them that bake regular, how much wood does the big oven rightly take to come to a bread heat and hold it. im asking because ours seems to eat half again what it used to and still comes up short, and im trying to work out if its the oven gone bad or were feeding it wrong. i brew more than i bake so i dont know the right of it. whats a proper fill for it, and how long should it sit before you draw the coals.

posts.title: [GENERAL] it is the flue not the wood get the sweep to it
posts.author: zion-roon-02
posts.body: it is the flue, i am near certain of it, and not the wood at all. i remember the same trouble the winter before last. the soot has not been swept out of that oven since before the cold set in, and a choked flue cannot draw, so the fire will not come up to a true heat no matter how well you bank it. i would have the sweep to it before anyone blames the wood. it is a plain thing to check and it costs nothing but an hours dirty work.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the wood off the new seller is grene and wont catch
posts.author: zion-hesk-02
posts.body: i reckon its the wood myself, not the oven. that new seller down the end is passing off grene wood as seasoned, i payed good money for a load and half of it jest smoulders and spits and wont catch. definately not a years drying in it. no wonder folks ovens wont heat if theyre burning wet stuff. im that annoyed i wont buy off him agin. break one open and the heart of its still damp.

comments.author: zion-boll-02
comments.body: three ruined bakes this week here and all, im sick of wasting the flour on it.

comments.author: zion-weld-02
comments.body: near double the wood it wanted last year, i counted the logs going in myself.

comments.author: zion-roon-02
comments.body: it is not the wood, it is the flue. that oven has not had a sweep since the cold came on and a choked flue will not draw the heat up. i would clear it before i bought new wood.

comments.author: zion-lunt-02
comments.body: no roon, the flue were swept at candlemas, i did it mysen, so it int that. theres nowt up there. its the floor gone cold, it wont hold owt now however you bank it.

comments.author: zion-roon-02
comments.body: then it is the floor bricks cracked, which is worse and no quick mend. i am only saying look before you spend on wood you may not need.

comments.author: zion-lunt-02
comments.body: fair, could be the bricks, i never thought to look at the floor. still no bake out of it till its sorted, either road.

comments.author: zion-cade-02
comments.body: mine come out heavy and pale as clay all week, wich is no good for market.

comments.author: zion-yance-02
comments.body: same here, loaves like bricks, the wife blames me but its the oven not me.

comments.author: zion-dill-02
comments.body: did the oven ever come right after they re-laid the floor two winters back?

comments.author: zion-mund-02
comments.body: sweeping wont fix a cracked floor tho, mine went the same and it were the bricks probly, not the soot at all.

comments.author: zion-dunt-02
comments.body: green you say hesk, where are you getting that from, did you split a log and look or does it just not catch for you.

comments.author: zion-tass-02
comments.body: i had a load off that seller and all, wet as a bog, took an age to catch.

comments.author: zion-crane-02
comments.body: that shared handcart i booked for friday, someones took it a day early again, second time this month now.
